items.title: USS Iwo Jima (LHD 7) Ushers in New Leadership during Change of Command
items.description:
NORFOLK – Wasp-class amphibious assault ship USS Iwo Jima (LHD 7) held a change of command ceremony aboard Naval Station Norfolk, July 23.

During the ceremony, Capt. Kathryn Wijnaldum relieved Capt. Richard Haley as USS Iwo Jima’s (LHD 7) 19th commanding officer.

items.title: Anesthesiologist serves as NMRTC Twentynine Palms 'CO for a Day'
items.description: Navy Medicine Readiness and Training Command Twentynine Palms staff anesthesiologist Lt. Tristan McLean Loesberg participated in the command's "Commanding Officer for a Day" program on June 16, gaining firsthand insight into the leadership, decision-making and operational responsibilities of commanding a military treatment facility. carriers.title: B-Roll: USS Theodore Roosevelt leads RIMPAC 2026 multinational group sail
carriers.description: The Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Theodore Roosevelt (CVN 71) leads a group sail July 22, 2026, in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of Hawaii during Exercise Rim of the Pacific 2026. Thirty nations, 30 ships, five submarines, 15 national land forces, more than 190 aircraft and more than 30,000 personnel are participating in RIMPAC in and around the Hawaiian Islands, June 24 to July 31. carriers.title: Ford Carrier Strike Group visit underscores Lakehurst’s enduring partnership with the fleet
carriers.description: After a historic 11-month deployment senior leaders from USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) visited NAWCAD Lakehurst on July 15 to thank the New Jersey personnel for their support and explore new ways to work together before the ship returns to sea.
